```typescript
          ref={groupRef as any}
          position={position} 
          rotation={rotation} 
          scale={scale} 
          onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onClick(); }}
        >
          <boxGeometry args={[1, 1, 1]} />
          <meshStandardMaterial color={isSelected ? '#8b5cf6' : '#666'} />
        </mesh>
        {isSelected && transformMode && !locked && groupRef.current && (
          <TransformControls
            ref={transformRef}
            object={groupRef.current}
            mode={transformMode}
            onMouseUp={handleTransformEnd}
            size={0.75}
          />
        )}
      </group>
    );
  }
  
  return (
    <group>
      <group 
        ref={groupRef}
        position={position} 
        rotation={rotation} 
        scale={scale}
        onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onClick(); }}
      >
        <Center>
          <primitive object={clonedScene} />
        </Center>
        {isSelected && (
          <mesh>
            <boxGeometry args={[1.1, 1.1, 1.1]} />
            <meshBasicMaterial color="#8b5cf6" wireframe transparent opacity={0.3} />
          </mesh>
        )}
      </group>
      
      {/* Selection ring on ground */}
      {isSelected && (
        <mesh rotation={[-Math.PI / 2, 0, 0]} position={[position[0], 0.01, position[2]]}>
          <ringGeometry args={[0.8, 1, 32]} />
          <meshBasicMaterial color="#8b5cf6" transparent opacity={0.4} side={THREE.DoubleSide} />
        </mesh>
      )}
      
      {/* Transform Controls - only show when object is selected and not locked */}
      {isSelected && transformMode && !locked && groupRef.current && (
        <TransformControls
          ref={transformRef}
          object={groupRef.current}
          mode={transformMode}
          onMouseUp={handleTransformEnd}
          size={0.75}
        />
      )}
    </group>
  );
}

// GLB Model Wrapper with error handling
function SafeGLBModel(props: Parameters<typeof GLBModel>[0]) {
  const [hasError, setHasError] = useState(false);
  
  if (hasError) {
    return (
      <mesh position={props.position} rotation={props.rotation} scale={props.scale}>
        <boxGeometry args={[1, 1, 1]} />
        <meshStandardMaterial color="#ff6b6b" />
      </mesh>
    );
  }
  
  return (
    <Suspense fallback={
      <mesh position={props.position} rotation={props.rotation} scale={props.scale}>
        <boxGeometry args={[0.5, 0.5, 0.5]} />
        <meshStandardMaterial color="#8b5cf6" wireframe />
      </mesh>
    }>
      <GLBModel {...props} />
    </Suspense>
  );
}

// Individual 3D object with transform controls
function SceneObject3D({ 
  object, 
  isSelected, 
  onClick,
  activeTool,
  onTransformChange,
  orbitControlsRef,
  onTransformStart,
  onTransformEnd,
  renderMode,
  xrayMode,
}: { 
  object: SceneObject; 
  isSelected: boolean;
  onClick: () => void;
  activeTool: string;
  onTransformChange: (id: string, position: [number, number, number], rotation: [number, number, number], scale: [number, number, number]) => void;
  orbitControlsRef: React.RefObject<any>;
  onTransformStart: () => void;
  onTransformEnd: () => void;
  renderMode: RenderMode;
  xrayMode: boolean;
}) {
  const meshRef = useRef<THREE.Mesh>(null);
  const transformRef = useRef<any>(null);
  const [hovered, setHovered] = useState(false);
  
  // Get transform mode from active tool
  const getTransformMode = (): 'translate' | 'rotate' | 'scale' | undefined => {
    switch (activeTool) {
      case 'move': return 'translate';
      case 'rotate': return 'rotate';
      case 'scale': return 'scale';
      default: return undefined;
    }
  };
  
  const transformMode = getTransformMode();
  
  // Disable orbit controls while transforming - notify parent component
  useEffect(() => {
    if (transformRef.current) {
      const controls = transformRef.current;
      const callback = (event: any) => {
        if (event.value) {
          onTransformStart();
        } else {
          onTransformEnd();
        }
      };
      controls.addEventListener('dragging-changed', callback);
      return () => controls.removeEventListener('dragging-changed', callback);
    }
  }, [onTransformStart, onTransformEnd]);
  
  // Handle transform end
  const handleTransformEnd = () => {
    if (meshRef.current) {
      const pos = meshRef.current.position;
      const rot = meshRef.current.rotation;
      const scl = meshRef.current.scale;
      onTransformChange(
        object.id,
        [pos.x, pos.y, pos.z],
        [rot.x, rot.y, rot.z],
        [scl.x, scl.y, scl.z]
      );
    }
  };
  
  if (!object.visible) return null;
  
  const selectedColor = new THREE.Color(object.color).lerp(new THREE.Color('#ffffff'), 0.3).getHexString();
  
  // Determine opacity based on xray mode
  const isXrayTarget = xrayMode && isSelected;
  const opacity = isXrayTarget ? 0.25 : 1;
  const isWireframe = renderMode === 'wireframe';
  const isXrayAll = renderMode === 'xray';
  const isPoints = renderMode === 'points';
  const isFlat = renderMode === 'flat';
  const isNormal = renderMode === 'normal';
  
  // For points mode, render as point cloud
  if (isPoints) {
    return (
      <points
        position={object.position}
        rotation={object.rotation}
        scale={object.scale}
        onClick={(e: any) => { e.stopPropagation(); onClick(); }}
      >
        {getGeometry(object.type)}
        <pointsMaterial 
          color={isSelected ? `#${selectedColor}` : object.color}
          size={0.05}
          sizeAttenuation
        />
      </points>
    );
  }
  
  return (
    <group>
      <mesh 
        ref={meshRef}
        position={object.position}
        rotation={object.rotation}
        scale={object.scale}
        castShadow={!isWireframe && !isXrayAll && !isFlat && !isNormal}
        receiveShadow={!isWireframe && !isXrayAll && !isFlat && !isNormal}
        onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onClick(); }}
        onPointerOver={() => setHovered(true)}
        onPointerOut={() => setHovered(false)}
      >
        {getGeometry(object.type)}
        {isWireframe ? (
          <meshBasicMaterial 
            color={isSelected ? `#${selectedColor}` : object.color}
            wireframe
          />
        ) : isFlat ? (
          <meshLambertMaterial 
            color={isSelected ? `#${selectedColor}` : object.color}
            flatShading
          />
        ) : isNormal ? (
          <meshNormalMaterial />
        ) : (
          <meshStandardMaterial 
            color={isSelected ? `#${selectedColor}` : object.color} 
            metalness={object.metalness}
            roughness={object.roughness}
            emissive={hovered ? object.color : '#000000'}
            emissiveIntensity={hovered ? 0.15 : 0}
            transparent={isXrayTarget || isXrayAll}
            opacity={isXrayTarget ? 0.25 : (isXrayAll ? 0.6 : 1)}
          />
        )}
      </mesh>
      
      {/* Selection wireframe */}
      {isSelected && (
        <mesh 
          position={object.position}
          rotation={object.rotation}
          scale={object.scale.map(s => s * 1.02) as [number, number, number]}
        >
          {getGeometry(object.type)}
          <meshBasicMaterial color="#ffffff" wireframe transparent opacity={0.5} />
        </mesh>
      )}
      
      {/* Selection ring on ground */}
      {isSelected && (
        <mesh rotation={[-Math.PI / 2, 0, 0]} position={[object.position[0], 0.01, object.position[2]]}>
          <ringGeometry args={[0.8, 1, 32]} />
          <meshBasicMaterial color="#8b5cf6" transparent opacity={0.4} side={THREE.DoubleSide} />
        </mesh>
      )}
      
      {/* Transform Controls - only show when object is selected and not locked */}
      {isSelected && transformMode && !object.locked && meshRef.current && (
        <TransformControls
          ref={transformRef}
          object={meshRef.current}
          mode={transformMode}
          onMouseUp={handleTransformEnd}
          size={0.75}
        />
      )}
    </group>
  );
}

// All scene objects
function SceneObjects({ 
  objects,
  selectedId, 
  onSelect,
  activeTool,
  crossCanvasPaint,
  onAnyPaintStart,
  onAnyPaintEnd,
  onTransformChange,
  orbitControlsRef,
  onTransformStart,
  onTransformEnd,
  renderMode,
  xrayMode,
  paintColor,
  paintBrushSize,
  paintOpacity,
  paintAngle,
}: { 
  objects: SceneObject[];
  selectedId: string | null;
  onSelect: (id: string | null) => void;
  activeTool: string;
  crossCanvasPaint?: Viewport3DProps['crossCanvasPaint'];
  onAnyPaintStart?: (objectId: string) => void;
  onAnyPaintEnd?: () => void;
  onTransformChange: (id: string, position: [number, number, number], rotation: [number, number, number], scale: [number, number, number]) => void;
  orbitControlsRef: React.RefObject<any>;
  onTransformStart: () => void;
  onTransformEnd: () => void;
  renderMode: RenderMode;
  xrayMode: boolean;
  paintColor: string;
  paintBrushSize: number;
  paintOpacity: number;
  paintAngle: number;
}) {
  return (
    <>
      {objects.map(obj => {
        // Handle GLB models separately
        if (obj.type === 'glb' && obj.modelUrl) {
          return (
            <SafeGLBModel
              key={obj.id}
              url={obj.modelUrl}
              position={obj.position}
              rotation={obj.rotation}
              scale={obj.scale}
              isSelected={selectedId === obj.id}
              onClick={() => onSelect(obj.id)}
              activeTool={activeTool}
              onTransformChange={onTransformChange}
              objectId={obj.id}
              orbitControlsRef={orbitControlsRef}
              onTransformStart={onTransformStart}
              onTransformEnd={onTransformEnd}
              locked={obj.locked}
            />
          );
        }
        
        // Regular objects use PaintableSceneObject3D
        return (
          <PaintableSceneObject3D 
            key={obj.id}
            object={obj}
            isSelected={selectedId === obj.id}
            onClick={() => onSelect(obj.id)}
            activeTool={activeTool}
            crossCanvasPaint={crossCanvasPaint}
            onTransformChange={onTransformChange}
            orbitControlsRef={orbitControlsRef}
            onTransformStart={onTransformStart}
            onTransformEnd={onTransformEnd}
            renderMode={renderMode}
            xrayMode={xrayMode}
            paintColor={paintColor}
            paintBrushSize={paintBrushSize}
            paintOpacity={paintOpacity}
            paintAngle={paintAngle}
            onPaintStart={onAnyPaintStart}
            onPaintEnd={onAnyPaintEnd}
          />
        );
      })}
      
      {/* Ground plane */}
      <mesh 
        rotation={[-Math.PI / 2, 0, 0]} 
        position={[0, 0, 0]} 
        receiveShadow
        onClick={() => onSelect(null)}
      >
        <planeGeometry args={[50, 50]} />
        <meshStandardMaterial 
          color="#2a2a2a" 
          transparent 
          opacity={0.3}
        />
      </mesh>
    </>
  );
}

// ============================================================================
// PERFORMANCE STATS (FPS, Memory, etc)
// ============================================================================

function PerformanceStats({ visible }: { visible: boolean }) {
  const [stats, setStats] = useState({ fps: 0, triangles: 0, calls: 0 });
  const { gl, scene } = useThree();
  
  useFrame(() => {
    if (!visible) return;
    
    const info = gl.info;
    setStats({
      fps: Math.round(1000 / (performance.now() % 1000)),
      triangles: info.render?.triangles || 0,
      calls: info.render?.calls || 0,
    });
  });
  
  if (!visible) return null;
  
  return null; // Stats are rendered in overlay
}

// ============================================================================
// CAMERA CONTROLLER
// ============================================================================

function CameraController({ 
  preset,
  orbitControlsRef,
}: { 
  preset: CameraPreset;
  orbitControlsRef: React.RefObject<any>;
}) {
  const { camera } = useThree();
  
  useEffect(() => {
    if (preset && CAMERA_PRESETS[preset]) {
      const pos = CAMERA_PRESETS[preset].position;
      camera.position.set(pos[0], pos[1], pos[2]);
      camera.lookAt(0, 0, 0);
      // Update orbit controls target
      if (orbitControlsRef.current) {
        orbitControlsRef.current.target.set(0, 0, 0);
        orbitControlsRef.current.update();
      }
    }
  }, [preset, camera, orbitControlsRef]);
  
  return null; // OrbitControls are rendered elsewhere
}

// ============================================================================
// SCENE CONTENT
// ============================================================================

function SceneContent({
  lightingMode,
  toolSettings,
  selectedObjectId,
  onSelectObject,
  cameraPreset,
  objects,
  activeTool,
  crossCanvasPaint,
  isPainting,
  paintLockObjectId,
  onAnyPaintStart,
  onAnyPaintEnd,
  onTransformChange,
  orbitControlsRef,
  onTransformStart,
  onTransformEnd,
  renderMode,
  xrayMode,
  surfaceBrushSize,
  onBrushSizeChange,
  paintColor,
  paintOpacity,
  paintAngle,
  onPaintAngleChange,
  ghostPosition,
  ghostSnapOption,
  ghostEnabled,
  onSceneReady,
  brushSettings,
  onBrushSettingsChange,
  showTopologyGrid = false,
  gridResolution = 16,
  // Ruler system props
  showRulers = true,
  guidePlanes = [],
  onAddGuidePlane,
  onUpdateGuidePlane,
  onDeleteGuidePlane,
  activeRuler,
  rulerHoverPosition,
  onRulerHover,
  onRulerClick,
  showRulerPreviewPlanes = false, // Default to false - only edge rulers show preview by default
  highlightedPlaneId, // External highlight control
  onSurfaceCursorStateChange, // Callback for cursor state changes
  referencePack, // ReferencePack for ortho image overlay
  baseMeshGeometry, // Base mesh geometry from Curve-First Generator
}: {
  lightingMode: 'day' | 'night';
  toolSettings: Viewport3DProps['toolSettings'];
  selectedObjectId: string | null;
  onSelectObject: (id: string | null) => void;
  cameraPreset: CameraPreset;
  objects: SceneObject[];
  activeTool: string;
  crossCanvasPaint?: Viewport3DProps['crossCanvasPaint'];
  isPainting?: boolean;
  paintLockObjectId?: string | null;
  onAnyPaintStart?: (objectId: string) => void;
  onAnyPaintEnd?: () => void;
  onTransformChange: (id: string, position: [number, number, number], rotation: [number, number, number], scale: [number, number, number]) => void;
  orbitControlsRef: React.RefObject<any>;
  onTransformStart: () => void;
  onTransformEnd: () => void;
  renderMode: RenderMode;
  xrayMode: boolean;
  surfaceBrushSize: number;
  onBrushSizeChange: (size: number) => void;
  paintColor: string;
  paintOpacity: number;
  paintAngle: number;
  onPaintAngleChange: (angle: number) => void;
  ghostPosition?: THREE.Vector3 | null;
  ghostSnapOption?: string | null;
  ghostEnabled?: boolean;
  onSceneReady?: (scene: THREE.Scene) => void;
  brushSettings?: BrushSettings;
  onBrushSettingsChange?: (settings: Partial<BrushSettings>) => void;
  showTopologyGrid?: boolean;
  gridResolution?: number;
  // Ruler system
  showRulers?: boolean;
  guidePlanes?: GuidePlane[];
  onAddGuidePlane?: (plane: any) => void;
  onUpdateGuidePlane?: (id: string, updates: Partial<GuidePlane>) => void;
  onDeleteGuidePlane?: (id: string) => void;
  activeRuler?: 'x' | 'y' | 'z' | null;
  rulerHoverPosition?: number | null;
  onRulerHover?: (axis: 'x' | 'y' | 'z' | null, position: number | null) => void;
  onRulerClick?: (axis: 'x' | 'y' | 'z', position: number) => void;
  showRulerPreviewPlanes?: boolean; // Toggle for in-view hover preview planes
  highlightedPlaneId?: string | null; // External highlight control
  referencePack?: import('../modelmaker/reference-pack').ReferencePack | null; // ReferencePack for ortho image overlay
}) {
  const { scene } = useThree();
  
  // Provide scene reference to parent and initialize SnapEngine
  useEffect(() => {
    if (onSceneReady) {
      onSceneReady(scene);
    }
    // Initialize SnapEngine with scene and container bounds
    const engine = SnapEngine.getInstance();
    engine.setScene(scene);
    engine.setContainer(new THREE.Box3(
      new THREE.Vector3(-50, 0, -50),
      new THREE.Vector3(50, 50, 50)
    ));
  }, [scene, onSceneReady]);
  
  // Get selected object mesh for ghost preview
  const selectedMesh = useMemo(() => {
    if (!selectedObjectId) return null;
    const obj = objects.find(o => o.id === selectedObjectId);
    if (!obj) return null;
    
    // Create a mesh for the ghost preview
    const mesh = new THREE.Mesh();
    mesh.position.fromArray(obj.position);
    mesh.rotation.set(obj.rotation[0], obj.rotation[1], obj.rotation[2]);
    mesh.scale.fromArray(obj.scale);
    
    // Create geometry based on type
    switch (obj.type) {
      case 'cube':
        mesh.geometry = new THREE.BoxGeometry(1, 1, 1);
        break;
      case 'sphere':
        mesh.geometry = new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.5, 32, 32);
        break;
      case 'cylinder':
        mesh.geometry = new THREE.CylinderGeometry(0.5, 0.5, 1, 32);
        break;
      case 'cone':
        mesh.geometry = new THREE.ConeGeometry(0.5, 1, 32);
        break;
      case 'torus':
        mesh.geometry = new THREE.TorusGeometry(0.5, 0.2, 16, 32);
        break;
      case 'plane':
        mesh.geometry = new THREE.PlaneGeometry(1, 1);
        break;
    }
    
    // Add material
    mesh.material = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: obj.color });
    mesh.uuid = obj.id;
    
    return mesh;
  }, [selectedObjectId, objects]);
  return (
    <>
      {/* Sky */}
      <SkyBackground mode={lightingMode} />
      
      {/* Lighting */}
      <Lighting mode={lightingMode} />
      
      {/* Grid */}
      <GridSystem visible={toolSettings.snapToGrid} size={toolSettings.gridSize} />
      
      {/* ReferencePack System - Ortho Image Overlay (Modelmaker Phase 1) */}
      {referencePack && (
        <Suspense fallback={null}>
          <ReferencePackManager
            pack={referencePack}
            baseUrl="/reference_packs/"
          />
        </Suspense>
      )}
      
      {/* Curve-First Generator - Base Mesh (Modelmaker Phase 1) */}
      {baseMeshGeometry && (
        <mesh geometry={baseMeshGeometry}>
          <meshStandardMaterial 
            color="#8b5cf6" 
            wireframe={true}
            metalness={0.3}
            roughness={0.4}
          />
        </mesh>
      )}
      
      {/* 3D Rulers (World Space) */}
      {showRulers && onAddGuidePlane && onUpdateGuidePlane && onDeleteGuidePlane && onRulerHover && onRulerClick && (
        <Ruler3DSystem
          guidePlanes={guidePlanes as any[]}
          onAddGuidePlane={onAddGuidePlane}
          onUpdateGuidePlane={onUpdateGuidePlane}
          onDeleteGuidePlane={onDeleteGuidePlane}
          activeRuler={activeRuler ?? null}
          onRulerHover={onRulerHover}
          onRulerClick={onRulerClick}
          showPreviewPlanes={toolSettings?.showRulerPreviewPlanes === true}
          highlightedPlaneId={highlightedPlaneId}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* 3D Cursor */}
      <Cursor3D 
        enabled={toolSettings.cursorEnabled}
        showAxisLines={toolSettings.cursorShowAxisLines}
        snapToGrid={toolSettings.snapToGrid}
        gridSize={toolSettings.gridSize}
      />
      
      {/* Surface Paint Cursor */}
      <SurfaceCursor
        enabled={activeTool === 'surfacePaint' || (activeTool === 'paint' && crossCanvasPaint?.enabled === true)}
        brushSize={
          activeTool === 'paint' && crossCanvasPaint?.enabled === true
            ? Math.max(0.1, Math.min(5, crossCanvasPaint.brushSize * 0.01))
            : surfaceBrushSize
        }
        onBrushSizeChange={
          activeTool === 'paint' && crossCanvasPaint?.enabled === true
            ? () => {}
            : onBrushSizeChange
        }
        objects={objects}
        angle={paintAngle}
        onAngleChange={onPaintAngleChange}
        lockObjectId={(isPainting ?? false) ? (paintLockObjectId ?? selectedObjectId) : null}
        preview={
          activeTool === 'paint' &&
          crossCanvasPaint?.enabled === true &&
          crossCanvasPaint.sourceCanvas &&
          crossCanvasPaint.anchor
            ? {
                sourceCanvas: crossCanvasPaint.sourceCanvas,
                sourcePoint: crossCanvasPaint.anchor.canvas,
                brushSizePx: crossCanvasPaint.brushSize,
              }
            : null
        }
        onStateChange={onSurfaceCursorStateChange}
      />
      
      {/* Scene Objects */}
      <SceneObjects 
        objects={objects}
        selectedId={selectedObjectId}
        onSelect={onSelectObject}
        activeTool={activeTool}
        crossCanvasPaint={crossCanvasPaint}
        onAnyPaintStart={onAnyPaintStart}
        onAnyPaintEnd={onAnyPaintEnd}
        onTransformChange={onTransformChange}
        orbitControlsRef={orbitControlsRef}
        onTransformStart={onTransformStart}
        onTransformEnd={onTransformEnd}
        renderMode={renderMode}
        xrayMode={xrayMode}
        paintColor={paintColor}
        paintBrushSize={surfaceBrushSize}
        paintOpacity={paintOpacity}
        paintAngle={paintAngle}
      />
      
      {/* Ghost Preview for Snap System */}
      {ghostEnabled && ghostPosition && selectedMesh && (
        <GhostPreviewRenderer
          originalObject={selectedMesh}
          targetPosition={ghostPosition}
          snapOption={ghostSnapOption as any}
          scene={scene}
          opacity={0.5}
          enableCollisionDetection={true}
          enableMeasurements={true}
          onRenderComplete={() => {}}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Snap Alignment Guides */}
      {selectedMesh && ghostEnabled && ghostPosition && (
        <SnapGuidesRenderer
          objectPosition={selectedMesh.position}
          targetPosition={ghostPosition}
          otherObjects={scene.children.filter(
            child => 
              child.type === 'Mesh' && 
              child.uuid !== selectedMesh.uuid &&
              child.visible
          )}
          alignmentThreshold={0.3}
          gridSize={toolSettings.gridSize}
          enabled={true}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Mesh Editing System (Sculpt Tool) */}
      {activeTool === 'sculpt' && selectedMesh && brushSettings && (
        <EditableMesh
          object={selectedMesh}
          brushSettings={brushSettings}
          onMeshChange={(geometry) => {
            // Update geometry in parent
            selectedMesh.geometry = geometry;
          }}
          showTopologyGrid={showTopologyGrid}
          gridResolution={gridResolution}
          enabled={true}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Gizmo Helper (corner axis indicator) */}
      <GizmoHelper alignment="bottom-right" margin={[80, 80]}>
        <GizmoViewport 
          axisColors={['#ef4444', '#22c55e', '#3b82f6']} 
          labelColor="white"
        />
      </GizmoHelper>
      
      {/* Camera Controller - just handles preset changes */}
      <CameraController preset={cameraPreset} orbitControlsRef={orbitControlsRef} />
    </>
  );
}

// ============================================================================
// VIEWPORT OVERLAY (UI controls on top of canvas)
// ============================================================================

function ViewportOverlay(_props: {
  lightingMode: 'day' | 'night';
  onLightingModeChange: (mode: 'day' | 'night') => void;
  showGrid: boolean;
  showCursor: boolean;
  cameraPreset: CameraPreset;
  onCameraPresetChange: (preset: CameraPreset) => void;
  showStats: boolean;
  onToggleStats: () => void;
  stats: { fps: number; triangles: number; calls: number };
  renderMode: RenderMode;
  onRenderModeChange: (mode: RenderMode) => void;
  xrayMode: boolean;
  onXrayModeChange: (enabled: boolean) => void;
  selectedObjectId: string | null;
  // Quick Snap Toolbar props
  gridSize?: number;
  onGridSizeChange?: (size: number) => void;
  gridSnapEnabled?: boolean;
  onGridSnapToggle?: (enabled: boolean) => void;
  onSnapClick?: (option: SnapOption) => void;
  onSnapHover?: (option: SnapOption | null) => void;
  showQuickSnap?: boolean;
  showRulerPreviewPlanes?: boolean;
  onToggleRulerPreviewPlanes?: () => void;
}) {
  // Viewport overlay is now empty - all buttons moved to bottom bar
  void _props;
  return null;
}

// ============================================================================
// MAIN COMPONENT
// ============================================================================

// Default objects if none provided
const DEFAULT_OBJECTS: SceneObject[] = [
  { id: 'obj-1', type: 'cube', position: [0, 0.5, 0], rotation: [0, 0, 0], scale: [1, 1, 1], color: '#8b5cf6', metalness: 0.3, roughness: 0.4, name: 'Cube', visible: true, locked: false },
  { id: 'obj-2', type: 'sphere', position: [2.5, 0.5, 0], rotation: [0, 0, 0], scale: [1, 1, 1], color: '#10b981', metalness: 0.5, roughness: 0.2, name: 'Sphere', visible: true, locked: false },
  { id: 'obj-3', type: 'cylinder', position: [-2.5, 0.5, 0], rotation: [0, 0, 0], scale: [1, 1, 1], color: '#f59e0b', metalness: 0.4, roughness: 0.3, name: 'Cylinder', visible: true, locked: false },
  { id: 'obj-4', type: 'cone', position: [0, 0.75, 2.5], rotation: [0, 0, 0], scale: [1, 1, 1], color: '#ef4444', metalness: 0.3, roughness: 0.5, name: 'Cone', visible: true, locked: false },
  { id: 'obj-5', type: 'torus', position: [0, 0.5, -2.5], rotation: [0, 0, 0], scale: [1, 1, 1], color: '#06b6d4', metalness: 0.6, roughness: 0.2, name: 'Torus', visible: true, locked: false },
];

// Render mode types
type RenderMode = 'solid' | 'wireframe' | 'xray' | 'points' | 'flat' | 'normal';
type RenderQuality = 'draft' | 'standard' | 'high' | 'ultra';

// Quality presets
const QUALITY_PRESETS: Record<RenderQuality, { shadows: boolean; antialias: boolean; bloom: boolean; ao: boolean; label: string; icon: string }> = {
  draft: { shadows: false, antialias: false, bloom: false, ao: false, label: 'Draft', icon: '⚡' },
  standard: { shadows: true, antialias: true, bloom: false, ao: false, label: 'Standard', icon: '🎯' },
  high: { shadows: true, antialias: true, bloom: true, ao: true, label: 'High', icon: '✨' },
  ultra: { shadows: true, antialias: true, bloom: true, ao: true, label: 'Ultra', icon: '🌟' },
};

// Right-click + scroll zoom handler
// Manually handles zoom when right-click is held + wheel is scrolled
function RightClickZoomHandler({ 
  orbitControlsRef,
  enabled = true,
  onRightClickStateChange,
}: { 
  orbitControlsRef: React.RefObject<any>;
  enabled?: boolean;
  onRightClickStateChange?: (isHeld: boolean) => void;
}) {
  const { gl, camera } = useThree();
  const isRightClickHeldRef = useRef(false);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!enabled) return;
    
    const dom = gl.domElement;
    
    const handleMouseDown = (e: MouseEvent) => {
      if (e.button === 2) { // Right mouse button
        isRightClickHeldRef.current = true;
        onRightClickStateChange?.(true);
      }
    };
    
    const handleMouseUp = (e: MouseEvent) => {
      if (e.button === 2) {
        isRightClickHeldRef.current = false;
        onRightClickStateChange?.(false);
      }
    };
    
    const handleContextMenu = (e: MouseEvent) => {
      // Prevent browser context menu when right-clicking
      e.preventDefault();
    };
    
    const handleWheel = (e: WheelEvent) => {
      // Only zoom if right-click is held and event is over the canvas
      if (isRightClickHeldRef.current && orbitControlsRef.current) {
        const rect = dom.getBoundingClientRect();
        const isOverCanvas = (
          e.clientX >= rect.left &&
          e.clientX <= rect.right &&
          e.clientY >= rect.top &&
          e.clientY <= rect.bottom
        );
        
        if (isOverCanvas) {
          e.preventDefault();
          e.stopPropagation();
          e.stopImmediatePropagation();
          
          const controls = orbitControlsRef.current;
          const zoomSpeed = 0.2;
          const delta = e.deltaY > 0 ? zoomSpeed : -zoomSpeed; // Flipped: scroll down = zoom in, scroll up = zoom out
          
          // Directly manipulate camera distance from target
          if (controls && controls.object && controls.target) {
            const direction = new THREE.Vector3();
            direction.subVectors(controls.object.position, controls.target).normalize();
            const currentDistance = controls.object.position.distanceTo(controls.target);
            const newDistance = Math.max(1, Math.min(100, currentDistance + delta));
            
            // Update camera position
            controls.object.position.copy(controls.target).add(direction.multiplyScalar(newDistance));
            controls.update();
          }
        }
      }
    };
    
    // Listen for mouse events on the canvas
    dom.addEventListener('mousedown', handleMouseDown, true);
    dom.addEventListener('mouseup', handleMouseUp, true);
    window.addEventListener('mouseup', handleMouseUp, true); // Catch mouseup outside canvas
    dom.addEventListener('contextmenu', handleContextMenu);
    
    // Listen for wheel events on window to catch them even when OrbitControls is active
    window.addEventListener('wheel', handleWheel, { passive: false, capture: true });
    
    return () => {
      dom.removeEventListener('mousedown', handleMouseDown, true);
      dom.removeEventListener('mouseup', handleMouseUp, true);
      window.removeEventListener('mouseup', handleMouseUp, true);
      dom.removeEventListener('contextmenu', handleContextMenu);
      window.removeEventListener('wheel', handleWheel as any, true);
    };
  }, [enabled, gl, camera, orbitControlsRef, onRightClickStateChange]);
  
  return null;
}

// OrbitControls wrapper to get ref - accepts enabled prop to disable during transforms
function OrbitControlsWithRef({ 
  orbitRef,
  enabled = true,
}: { 
  orbitRef: React.RefObject<any>;
  enabled?: boolean;
}) {
  return (
    <OrbitControls
      ref={orbitRef}
      enabled={enabled}
      enableDamping
      dampingFactor={0.05}
      minDistance={1}
      maxDistance={100}
      // Disable default scroll zoom (we handle it manually with right-click + scroll)
      enableZoom={false}
      // Don't interfere with transform controls
      mouseButtons={{
        LEFT: enabled ? THREE.MOUSE.ROTATE : undefined,
        MIDDLE: enabled ? THREE.MOUSE.DOLLY : undefined,
        RIGHT: enabled ? THREE.MOUSE.PAN : undefined,
      }}
    />
  );
}

// Camera capture helper - notifies parent when camera is ready
function CameraCapture({ 
  cameraRef, 
  onReady 
}: { 
  cameraRef: React.MutableRefObject<THREE.Camera | null>; 
  onReady?: (camera: THREE.Camera) => void;
}) {
  const { camera } = useThree();
  
  useEffect(() => {
    if (cameraRef) {
      cameraRef.current = camera;
    }
    if (onReady) {
      onReady(camera);
    }
  }, [camera, cameraRef, onReady]);
  
  return null;
}

export function Viewport3D({
  lightingMode,
  onLightingModeChange,
  activeTool,
  toolSettings,
  onToolSettingsChange,
  selectedObjectId,
  onSelectObject,
  objects: externalObjects,
  onObjectsChange,
  onAddObject,
  onDeleteObject,
  crossCanvasPaint,
  ghostPosition,
  ghostSnapOption,
  ghostEnabled = false,
  onSceneReady,
  brushSettings,
  onBrushSettingsChange,
  showTopologyGrid = false,
  gridResolution = 16,
  showRulers = true,
  guidePlanes: externalGuidePlanes,
  onAddGuidePlane: externalAddGuidePlane,
  onUpdateGuidePlane: externalUpdateGuidePlane,
  onDeleteGuidePlane: externalDeleteGuidePlane,
  showRulerPreviewPlanes = false, // Default to false - only edge rulers show preview by default
  highlightedPlaneId, // External highlight control
  onCameraReady,
  onOrbitControlsReady,
  // External control props
  cameraPreset: externalCameraPreset,
  onCameraPresetChange: externalOnCameraPresetChange,
  renderMode: externalRenderMode,
  onRenderModeChange: externalOnRenderModeChange,
  xrayMode: externalXrayMode,
  onXrayModeChange: externalOnXrayModeChange,
  showStats: externalShowStats,
  onToggleStats: externalOnToggleStats,
  stats: externalStats,
  onStatsChange: externalOnStatsChange,
  onSurfaceCursorStateChange,
  onGuidePlanesChange,
  onGuidePlaneOperationsReady,
  referencePack,
  baseMeshGeometry,
}: Viewport3DProps & {
  brushSettings?: BrushSettings;
  onBrushSettingsChange?: (settings: Partial<BrushSettings>) => void;
  showTopologyGrid?: boolean;
  gridResolution?: number;
}) {
  // Use external props if provided, otherwise use internal state
  const [internalCameraPreset, setInternalCameraPreset] = useState<CameraPreset>('perspective');
  const [internalShowStats, setInternalShowStats] = useState(false);
  const [internalStats, setInternalStats] = useState({ fps: 60, triangles: 2500, calls: 15 });
  const [internalRenderMode, setInternalRenderMode] = useState<RenderMode>('solid');
  const [internalXrayMode, setInternalXrayMode] = useState(false);
  
  const cameraPreset = externalCameraPreset ?? internalCameraPreset;
  const setCameraPreset = externalOnCameraPresetChange ?? setInternalCameraPreset;
  const showStats = externalShowStats ?? internalShowStats;
  const setShowStats = externalOnToggleStats ?? (() => setInternalShowStats(s => !s));
  const stats = externalStats ?? internalStats;
  const renderMode = externalRenderMode ?? internalRenderMode;
  const setRenderMode = externalOnRenderModeChange ?? setInternalRenderMode;
  const xrayMode = externalXrayMode ?? internalXrayMode;
  const setXrayMode = externalOnXrayModeChange ?? setInternalXrayMode;
  
  const [isTransforming, setIsTransforming] = useState(false);
  const [isPainting, setIsPainting] = useState(false);
  const [paintLockObjectId, setPaintLockObjectId] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const [isRightClickHeld, setIsRightClickHeld] = useState(false);
  const orbitControlsRef = useRef<any>(null);
  const cameraRef = useRef<THREE.Camera | null>(null);
  
  // Guide planes state - use internal state if not provided externally
  const internalGuidePlanes = useGuidePlanes();
  const guidePlanes = externalGuidePlanes ?? internalGuidePlanes.planes;
  const addGuidePlane = externalAddGuidePlane ?? internalGuidePlanes.addPlane;
  const updateGuidePlane = externalUpdateGuidePlane ?? internalGuidePlanes.updatePlane;
  const deleteGuidePlane = externalDeleteGuidePlane ?? internalGuidePlanes.deletePlane;
  const clearAllPlanes = internalGuidePlanes.clearAllPlanes;
  
  // Expose guide planes to parent for bottom bar
  useEffect(() => {
    if (onGuidePlanesChange) {
      onGuidePlanesChange(guidePlanes);
    }
  }, [guidePlanes, onGuidePlanesChange]);
  
  // Expose guide plane operations to parent
  useEffect(() => {
    if (onGuidePlaneOperationsReady) {
      onGuidePlaneOperationsReady({
        deleteGuidePlane,
        clearAllGuidePlanes: clearAllPlanes,
      });
    }
  }, [onGuidePlaneOperationsReady, deleteGuidePlane, clearAllPlanes]);
  
  // Ruler hover state
  const [activeRuler, setActiveRuler] = useState<'x' | 'y' | 'z' | null>(null);
  const [rulerHoverPosition, setRulerHoverPosition] = useState<number | null>(null);
  
  // Surface cursor state (for bottom bar display)
  const [surfaceCursorState, setSurfaceCursorState] = useState<{
    isOnSurface: boolean;
    angle: number;
    brushSize: number;
    hitUV: { x: number; y: number } | null;
    surfaceObjectId: string | null;
  } | null>(null);
  
  // Expose cursor state to parent (clear when cursor disabled)
  useEffect(() => {
    if (onSurfaceCursorStateChange) {
      const isCursorEnabled = activeTool === 'surfacePaint' || (activeTool === 'paint' && crossCanvasPaint?.enabled === true);
      if (isCursorEnabled) {
        onSurfaceCursorStateChange(surfaceCursorState);
      } else {
        onSurfaceCursorStateChange(null);
      }
    }
  }, [surfaceCursorState, onSurfaceCursorStateChange, activeTool, crossCanvasPaint?.enabled]);
  
  // Notify parent when orbit controls are ready
  useEffect(() => {
    if (orbitControlsRef.current && onOrbitControlsReady) {
      onOrbitControlsReady(orbitControlsRef.current);
    }
  }, [orbitControlsRef.current, onOrbitControlsReady]);
  
  // Surface paint state (local state for interactive adjustment)
  const [surfaceBrushSize, setSurfaceBrushSize] = useState(toolSettings.surfacePaintBrushSize || 0.5);
  const [paintAngle, setPaintAngle] = useState(0); // Angle in degrees
  const paintColor = toolSettings.surfacePaintColor || '#8b5cf6';
  const paintOpacity = 0.8; // Could be made configurable
  
  // Update brush size in parent when it changes
  const handleBrushSizeChange = useCallback((size: number) => {
    setSurfaceBrushSize(size);
    if (onToolSettingsChange) {
      onToolSettingsChange({ surfacePaintBrushSize: size });
    }
  }, [onToolSettingsChange]);
  
  // Handle paint angle change (from SurfaceCursor scroll)
  const handlePaintAngleChange = useCallback((angle: number) => {
    setPaintAngle(angle);
  }, []);
  
  // Use external objects if provided, otherwise use defaults
  const objects = externalObjects || DEFAULT_OBJECTS;
  
  // Handle object selection
  const handleSelectObject = useCallback((id: string | null) => {
    if (onSelectObject) {
      onSelectObject(id);
    }
  }, [onSelectObject]);
  
  // Handle transform changes
  const handleTransformChange = useCallback((
    id: string, 
    position: [number, number, number], 
    rotation: [number, number, number], 
    scale: [number, number, number]
  ) => {
    if (onObjectsChange) {
      const newObjects = objects.map(obj => 
        obj.id === id ? { ...obj, position, rotation, scale } : obj
      );
      onObjectsChange(newObjects);
    }
  }, [objects, onObjectsChange]);
  
  return (
    <div className="w-full h-full relative bg-[#1a1a2e]">
      {/* Three.js Canvas */}
      <Canvas
        className="w-full h-full"
        shadows
        gl={{ 
          antialias: true, 
          alpha: false,
          powerPreference: 'high-performance',
        }}
        dpr={[1, 2]}
        camera={{ position: [5, 5, 5], fov: 50 }}
      >
        {/* Camera */}
        <PerspectiveCamera makeDefault position={[5, 5, 5]} fov={50} />
        
        {/* Capture camera for View History */}
        <CameraCapture cameraRef={cameraRef} onReady={onCameraReady} />
        
        {/* Orbit Controls - disabled during transform operations */}
        <OrbitControlsWithRef 
          orbitRef={orbitControlsRef} 
          enabled={!isTransforming}
        />
        
        {/* Right-click + scroll zoom handler */}
        <RightClickZoomHandler 
          orbitControlsRef={orbitControlsRef} 
          enabled={!isTransforming}
          onRightClickStateChange={setIsRightClickHeld}
        />
        
        {/* Scene content */}
        <SceneContent
          lightingMode={lightingMode}
          toolSettings={toolSettings}
          selectedObjectId={selectedObjectId}
          onSelectObject={handleSelectObject}
          cameraPreset={cameraPreset}
          objects={objects}
          activeTool={activeTool}
          crossCanvasPaint={crossCanvasPaint}
          isPainting={isPainting}
          paintLockObjectId={paintLockObjectId}
          onAnyPaintStart={(objectId) => {
            setIsPainting(true);
            setPaintLockObjectId(objectId);
          }}
          onAnyPaintEnd={() => {
            setIsPainting(false);
            setPaintLockObjectId(null);
          }}
          onTransformChange={handleTransformChange}
          orbitControlsRef={orbitControlsRef}
          onTransformStart={() => setIsTransforming(true)}
          onTransformEnd={() => setIsTransforming(false)}
          renderMode={renderMode}
          xrayMode={xrayMode}
          surfaceBrushSize={surfaceBrushSize}
          onBrushSizeChange={handleBrushSizeChange}
          paintColor={paintColor}
          paintOpacity={paintOpacity}
          paintAngle={paintAngle}
          onPaintAngleChange={handlePaintAngleChange}
          ghostPosition={ghostPosition}
          ghostSnapOption={ghostSnapOption}
          ghostEnabled={ghostEnabled}
          onSceneReady={onSceneReady}
          brushSettings={brushSettings}
          onBrushSettingsChange={onBrushSettingsChange}
          showTopologyGrid={showTopologyGrid}
          gridResolution={gridResolution}
          // Ruler system
          showRulers={showRulers}
          guidePlanes={guidePlanes as any}
          onAddGuidePlane={addGuidePlane as any}
          onUpdateGuidePlane={updateGuidePlane}
          onDeleteGuidePlane={deleteGuidePlane}
          activeRuler={activeRuler}
          rulerHoverPosition={rulerHoverPosition}
          onRulerHover={(axis, pos) => {
            setActiveRuler(axis);
            setRulerHoverPosition(pos);
          }}
          onRulerClick={(axis, pos) => {
            addGuidePlane({
              axis,
              position: pos,
              color: axis === 'x' ? '#ff4444' : axis === 'y' ? '#44ff44' : '#4444ff',
              opacity: 0.15,
              locked: false,
              visible: true,
              bounded: false,
              label: `${axis.toUpperCase()} = ${pos}`,
            } as any);
          }}
          showRulerPreviewPlanes={showRulerPreviewPlanes}
          highlightedPlaneId={highlightedPlaneId}
          onSurfaceCursorStateChange={(state) => {
            setSurfaceCursorState(state);
          }}
          referencePack={referencePack}
          baseMeshGeometry={baseMeshGeometry}
        />
      </Canvas>
      
      {/* Overlay UI */}
      <ViewportOverlay
        lightingMode={lightingMode}
        onLightingModeChange={onLightingModeChange}
        showGrid={toolSettings.snapToGrid}
        showCursor={toolSettings.cursorEnabled}
        cameraPreset={cameraPreset}
        onCameraPresetChange={setCameraPreset}
        showStats={showStats}
        onToggleStats={() => setShowStats()}
        stats={stats}
        renderMode={renderMode}
        onRenderModeChange={setRenderMode}
        xrayMode={xrayMode}
        onXrayModeChange={setXrayMode}
        selectedObjectId={selectedObjectId}
        gridSize={toolSettings.gridSize}
        gridSnapEnabled={toolSettings.snapToGrid}
        showQuickSnap={true}
        onSnapClick={(option) => {
          // Handle snap from quick toolbar
          if (selectedObjectId && ghostPosition) {
            // Apply snap using engine
            console.log('Quick snap:', option);
          }
        }}
        onSnapHover={(option) => {
          // Could trigger ghost preview from toolbar
          // For now just log
          if (option) console.log('Snap hover:', option);
        }}
        showRulerPreviewPlanes={toolSettings?.showRulerPreviewPlanes === true}
        onToggleRulerPreviewPlanes={() => {
          if (onToolSettingsChange) {
            const currentValue = toolSettings?.showRulerPreviewPlanes === true;
            onToolSettingsChange({ showRulerPreviewPlanes: !currentValue });
          }
        }}
      />
      
      {/* 3D Rulers - Screen Edge Overlay */}
      {showRulers && (
        <ScreenRulers
          guidePlanes={guidePlanes as any}
          onAddGuidePlane={addGuidePlane as any}
          onUpdateGuidePlane={updateGuidePlane}
          onDeleteGuidePlane={deleteGuidePlane}
          visible={showRulers}
          rulerSize={24}
          majorInterval={5}
          minorDivisions={5}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Add Object Overlay - REMOVED - Now in Assets Drawer */}
      
      {/* Delete Object Button - Keep for quick access */}
      {selectedObjectId && onDeleteObject && (
        <div className="absolute bottom-4 right-4 z-10">
          <button
            onClick={() => onDeleteObject(selectedObjectId)}
            className="p-2 rounded-lg bg-red-500/20 hover:bg-red-500/40 text-red-400 hover:text-red-300 transition-all border border-red-500/30 backdrop-blur-sm"
            title="Delete selected object (Del)"
          >
            <Trash2 className="w-4 h-4" />
          </button>
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

```



---


# Perfect Magnetic Lasso - Complete Mathematical Specification

**Purpose:** Complete mathematical and implementation specification for a perfect magnetic lasso system that preserves three critical invariants and uses advanced tracking algorithms.

**Status:** Complete Specification  
**Date:** 2025-01-27  
**Agent:** Image

---

## The Three Invariants

A perfect magnetic lasso must preserve three invariants:

### 1. Arc-Length Consistency
Points along the committed path are spaced by (approximately) a constant step in image space (scaled by zoom).
- Prevents density explosions
- Keeps the system stable

### 2. Alignment-Over-Position (Key Philosophy)
The lasso should prefer directions that match boundary flow (tangent), not a specific rail location.
- It "knows where to go" by orientation, not by snapping to a centerline
- Direction-first matching, not position-first

### 3. Temporal Continuity / Hysteresis
Once it chooses an edge hypothesis, it should not jump to a competitor unless the evidence is decisively better.
- Prevents flickering
- Maintains stable tracking

Everything below is built to protect these three invariants.

---

## 1. The First Click Problem (Seed Snapping Without Feeling Like Snapping)

The first click is rarely pixel-perfect. So the "start node" should be a seed estimate, not a literal click point.

### Seed Selection (Local MAP Estimate)

When the user clicks at \(c_0\), define a small search window \(W\) (radius 6–20 px depending on zoom). Choose:

\[s_0 = \arg\max_{x \in W} (B(x) \cdot Q(x) \cdot A(x))\]

Where:
- \(B(x)\) = boundary likelihood
- \(Q(x)\) = boundary confidence/coherence
- \(A(x)\) = "agreement with intent" (initially weak, then strong once motion starts)

**Important UX rule:** If the best score is below a threshold, don't move it. Seed stays at click. No surprises on blank regions.

### Micro-Intent for the First Click

You can cheaply infer initial intent from the first few pixels of mouse motion after down:
- Preferred direction \(\hat{v}\)
- Then define a "cone" of acceptable edge tangents around \(\hat{v}\)

This helps disambiguate multiple nearby edges at the start.

---

## 2. The "Bisect + Rotate" Idea: Make It a Principled Stepper

What you described is essentially a constant-step pivoted search.

Let:
- \(A\) = last committed node (anchor)
- \(C\) = cursor
- Choose a fixed step length \(L\) (zoom-scaled)

Instead of drawing \(A \to C\), you generate the next node \(N\) at distance \(L\) from \(A\) and then "rotate" it to land on the best boundary-consistent direction.

### The "Rotation" is a Circular Search

Define a circle:
\[\|N - A\| = L\]

Parameterize candidate points:
\[N(\theta) = A + L[\cos\theta, \sin\theta]\]

Now score each candidate by boundary + tangent alignment + continuity:

\[\text{score}(N) = \alpha B(N)Q(N) + \beta |\hat{t}(N) \cdot \hat{d}| - \gamma \kappa(\text{history}) - \delta \text{switchPenalty}\]

Where:
- \(\hat{d} = \frac{N - A}{\|N - A\|}\) (candidate step direction)
- \(\hat{t}(N)\) = local tangent direction field (from structure tensor)
- \(\kappa(\text{history})\) penalizes sudden curvature change vs the last few segments
- \(\text{switchPenalty}\) discourages jumping to a different edge hypothesis

**Key:** This uses alignment (dot with tangent), exactly what you want.

### Efficient "Bisect" Version

You don't need to brute-force all \(\theta\). Do a two-stage search:
1. Coarse sweep over maybe 24–48 angles (fast)
2. Pick the best 2–3 peaks and do golden-section / ternary refinement around them

That gives you your "super efficient" behavior.

### Why the Pivot Matters

The pivot at \(A\) preserves spacing (arc-length invariant) while letting the point search directionally. No stretching, no jitter.

---

## 3. Upgrading It: Don't Search the Whole Circle, Search a Cone

Humans don't teleport direction; they steer. So the rotation range should be limited around a predicted forward direction.

### Define Predicted Direction \(\hat{p}\)

From:
- Cursor velocity direction \(\hat{v}\)
- Previous segment direction \(\hat{d}_{\text{prev}}\)

Blend:
\[\hat{p} = \text{norm}(w_v \hat{v} + w_d \hat{d}_{\text{prev}})\]

Then only search \(\theta\) in a cone around \(\hat{p}\) (say ±60°). This prevents the system from "finding a good edge behind you."

---

## 4. Competing Edges: Treat It as a Multi-Hypothesis Tracker, Not a Greedy Choice

Your "competing lines" note is the heart of the problem. The solution is: maintain K hypotheses for the next node direction (K=2 or 3), but keep it calm.

### What is a Hypothesis?

A hypothesis is:
- The last node position
- The last direction
- An "edge identity" proxy (a running signature of boundary/tangent characteristics)
- Accumulated cost

### Each Step:
1. Generate top candidates (peaks in your cone search)
2. Extend each hypothesis with those candidates
3. Keep best K by total score + continuity penalties

That's basically a tiny beam search / Viterbi in streaming form.

### The "Edge Identity" Proxy (Prevents Jumping)

Even without explicit segmentation, you can build an identity from local measurements:
- Boundary strength profile across the normal (two-side contrast)
- Tangent direction stability
- Local color contrast across normal
- Gradient phase consistency

If a candidate edge's signature differs sharply, add a penalty unless the user's motion strongly supports switching.

---

## 5. Looking Back Multiple Nodes: Do It as Curvature + Frequency, But Grounded

Your intuition about "looking back several nodes and detecting a consistent wave" is basically: predict the next direction from recent curvature.

Use a short history window (3–8 nodes) and fit:
- A smoothed tangent direction
- An expected curvature trend

### Two Practical Forms:

**A) Simple, Powerful: Exponential Smoothing on Direction**

Keep a smoothed direction vector:
\[\hat{d}_s \leftarrow \text{norm}((1-\lambda)\hat{d}_s + \lambda\hat{d}_{\text{prev}})\]

Then penalize deviation:
\[\text{penalty} = 1 - |\hat{d}_s \cdot \hat{d}|\]

**B) Curvature Continuity Penalty**

Compute turning angle \(\Delta\theta\) each step and penalize changes in \(\Delta\theta\). This prevents edge flipping at parallel boundaries.

This gives you the "human expects smooth continuation" property without mystical assumptions.

---

## 6. Where the Cursor Fits (Intent Without "Rail Sticking")

The cursor should influence direction, not absolute position.

So define intent as a preferred direction field:
- \(\hat{v}\) from cursor velocity
- Also a weak "attention tube" around the cursor path only for candidate generation (not for snapping)

Then add to score:
\[+\eta |\hat{v} \cdot \hat{d}|\]

And crucially:
- If the user pulls across the normal hard (large deviation), reduce switchPenalty: they're intentionally lane-changing.

That implements "sticks to intent."

---

## 7. How Autopins Emerge Naturally from This Stepper

Once you're committing nodes every fixed \(L\), "autopins" are not separate objects. They're just commits.

- Manual pins = "hard commits" with detents for unzipping
- Auto commits = "soft commits" you can scrub through quickly

This makes unzipping feel inevitable.

---

## 8. Putting It Together in One Loop

At each frame (when drawing forward):

1. Update intent direction \(\hat{v}\) from cursor motion
2. If distance from last committed node < \(L\): only update preview
3. Else:
   - Predict \(\hat{p}\)
   - Cone-search candidates on circle radius \(L\) around anchor \(A\)
   - Score candidates using boundary + tangent alignment + continuity + hypothesis penalties
   - Update K hypotheses, choose primary
   - Commit next node \(N\)

### Preview Rendering:
- Committed path is stable
- Preview path is either:
  - A few committed steps ahead (fast) or
  - A single step chord \(A \to C\) in reposition/unzip modes (as we discussed)

---

## The Big Meta-Point

Your "rotate around axis" concept is a great embodiment of the correct principle:

**The lasso should solve for the next step direction under constraints, not chase the cursor's instantaneous position.**

That's why it feels like intent.

---

## Complete Implementation Specification

### 1. Precompute the Right Three Fields: \(B, T, Q\)

Everything becomes easy if you turn the image into a few "sensors" you can query extremely fast.

#### 1.1 Gradient and Structure Tensor (Orientation)

Compute image gradients \(G_x, G_y\) (Scharr or Sobel). Use luminance or linear RGB luminance.

Then build the structure tensor (smoothed locally):

\[J = \begin{bmatrix}
\langle G_x^2 \rangle & \langle G_x G_y \rangle \\
\langle G_x G_y \rangle & \langle G_y^2 \rangle
\end{bmatrix}\]

From \(J\), get:
- Dominant edge normal direction (eigenvector of largest eigenvalue)
- Edge tangent direction \(T(x)\) (perpendicular to normal)
- Coherence \(Q(x)\) (how "line-like" it is)

**Coherence:**
\[Q = \frac{\lambda_1 - \lambda_2}{\lambda_1 + \lambda_2 + \epsilon}\]

Ranges ~0 (texture/noise) to ~1 (clean edge).

#### 1.2 Boundary Likelihood \(B(x)\)

You want a "how likely is an edge here?" scalar. Use:
- Gradient magnitude \(M = \sqrt{G_x^2 + G_y^2}\)
- Non-maximum suppression along gradient direction (optional but strong)
- Then normalize locally (robust): compare to local median/MAD so lighting changes don't wreck you
- Clamp into [0,1]

So \(B(x)\) is high on true edges and low elsewhere.

#### 1.3 Confidence \(Q'(x)\) (Yes, Reuse Coherence)

This is your "is this edge stable enough to lock onto?" field. Use coherence plus a magnitude sanity:

\[Q'(x) = Q(x) \cdot \text{smoothstep}(m_{\text{low}}, m_{\text{high}}, M(x))\]

This prevents locking onto weak, noisy gradients.

**Net:**
- \(B(x)\) says "edge exists"
- \(T(x)\) says "edge direction"
- \(Q'(x)\) says "edge is reliable"

---

### 2. Add an "Edge Identity Signature" So You Don't Jump Rails

Parallel edges and competing boundaries are solved by giving edges a fingerprint.

At a candidate point \(p\), compute a small signature across the normal direction \(n(p)\) (from structure tensor):

Sample at offsets \(\pm r\) along normal:
- \(I_+\) = average color/luma at \(p + rn\)
- \(I_-\) = average color/luma at \(p - rn\)

Define:
- **Contrast:** \(C = \|I_+ - I_-\|\)
- **Polarity:** \(P = \text{sign}((I_+ - I_-) \cdot w)\) (pick w = luminance weight or principal direction)
- **Edge width proxy:** sample at multiple radii to see if it's a thin boundary or broad gradient

**Signature vector example:**
\[S(p) = [C, P, Q'(p), T_x(p), T_y(p)]\]

Then your tracker can say: "this candidate feels like the same edge as the last few steps" vs "this is a different edge even if nearby."

---

### 3. The Stepping Model: Constant Arc-Length Pivot Search (Your Axis Idea)

Let \(A\) be the last committed node. Step length \(L\) is constant in image space (scale with zoom).

#### 3.1 Predict a Forward Direction (Intent)

Cursor velocity gives intent direction \(\hat{v}\). Previous lasso direction gives inertia \(\hat{d}_{\text{prev}}\).

Blend:
\[\hat{p} = \text{norm}(w_v \hat{v} + w_d \hat{d}_{\text{prev}})\]

#### 3.2 Search on a Circle, But Only Within a Cone

Candidates are on:
\[p(\theta) = A + L[\cos\theta, \sin\theta]\]

But only evaluate \(\theta\) within \(\pm\phi\) around \(\hat{p}\) (cone). Typical \(\phi\) = 45°–70°.

This is your "rotate around axis" search, but controlled.

#### 3.3 Score Each Candidate by Five Terms

This is the heart.

Let:
- \(\hat{d} = \text{norm}(p - A)\) (candidate step direction)
- \(\hat{t}(p) = T(p)\) (edge tangent)
- \(\Delta\theta\) = turning angle vs previous direction

**Score:**
\[\text{Score}(p) = \alpha B(p)Q'(p) \text{(edge quality)} + \beta |\hat{t}(p) \cdot \hat{d}| \text{(alignment-first)} + \gamma |\hat{v} \cdot \hat{d}| \text{(cursor intent)} - \mu \text{CurvPenalty}(\Delta\theta) \text{(smoothness)} - \rho \text{SwitchPenalty}(S(p), S_{\text{track}}) \text{(edge identity lock)}\]

Where:
- **CurvPenalty** should punish changes in curvature, not curvature itself. A steady curve is fine; sudden kinks are bad.
- **SwitchPenalty** is low when candidate signature matches the current track signature.

This makes your system:
- Follow edges by existence + reliability
- Prefer tangent alignment
- Still obey intent
- Remain temporally stable

---

### 4. Multi-Hypothesis Tracking (K=2–3 is Enough)

Greedy choice fails at junctions. Track 2–3 "edge hypotheses" in parallel.

Each hypothesis stores:
- Last node \(A\)
- Last direction \(\hat{d}\)
- Accumulated score
- Running signature \(S_{\text{track}}\) (EMA over last few steps)
- A "stickiness" value

#### Update Step (Beam Search)

At each commit:
1. For each hypothesis, generate top M candidates (local maxima in cone search)
2. Extend hypothesis with each candidate
3. Keep best K by total score

Because each step is local, K=3 is plenty and fast.

---

### 5. The Edge Switching Law: When You're Allowed to Jump

You don't want twitchy switching; you want commitment until the user forces a change.

Define:
- \(H_0\) = currently active hypothesis
- \(H_1\) = best alternative
- Score difference: \(\Delta = \text{score}(H_1) - \text{score}(H_0)\)

**Switch only if:**
1. **Evidence margin:** \(\Delta > \Delta_{\text{min}}\)
2. **Persistence:** This has been true for N consecutive commits (or for T milliseconds)
3. **User intent supports it:** Cursor direction strongly favors the alternative, or cursor crosses the normal enough

That last one is big: switching should feel like the user asked for it.

A clean "intent to switch" detector:
- If cursor motion has a strong component across the current edge normal (lane change), reduce stickiness.

---

### 6. The "First Click Seed" Becomes Consistent with the Tracker

At click \(c_0\):
1. Find best seed \(s_0\) in a small disk by maximizing \(B \cdot Q'\)
2. Initialize track signature \(S_{\text{track}} = S(s_0)\)
3. Set \(\hat{d}\) undefined until motion begins, then bootstrap from cursor drift

This solves your "first point not pixel accurate" problem without user noticing.

---

### 7. Corridors and Parallel Edges: Solve with Identity + Curvature Continuity

In a corridor (two strong parallel edges), both have high \(B\) and high alignment. So what decides?

You use:
- Edge signature match (polarity/contrast across normal)
- Path continuity (curvature trend)
- Stickiness (don't flip unless there's strong reason)
- User intent (if they steer toward the other wall, allow switch)

This makes it behave like a human: stable by default, switchable deliberately.

---

### 8. "Preview vs Commit" So It Feels Immediate

You don't need to commit every frame. You commit every time cursor distance from last committed point exceeds \(L\).

**Between commits:**
- Show preview as:
  - One-step predicted candidate, or
  - A short 2–4 step lookahead using the same stepper (fast and looks magical)

**During unzip/reposition:**
- Freeze solver, preview becomes a chord (as we designed)

---

### 9. Parameter Defaults That Tend to Feel "Photoshop-Pro" Immediately

- **Step length \(L\):** 6–10 px at 100% zoom, scale by \(1/\text{zoom}\)
- **Cone half-angle \(\phi\):** 60°
- **Candidates per step:** Coarse 32 angles + refine top 2 peaks
- **K hypotheses:** 3
- **Switch persistence:** 3 commits (or ~80–120ms)
- **Signature smoothing (EMA):** \(\lambda = 0.25\) (stable but responsive)

---

### 10. How Your "Tint Overlay Segmentation" Supercharges This

If you have a tint-label map or Δ-based label map behind the scenes, you can build a semantic boundary field:

- Boundaries between labels become extremely clean \(B(x)\)
- Orientation \(T(x)\) can come from label contours (even better than raw gradients)
- Confidence \(Q'(x)\) is high at label edges, low elsewhere

That gives you "perfect edges" even when the photo gradient is ambiguous.

---

## Implementation Details

### Cone Sampling + Peak Detection (Fast, Stable, No Jitter)

You have a 1D function over angle:
\[f(\theta) = \text{Score}(p(\theta)), \quad p(\theta) = A + L[\cos\theta, \sin\theta]\]

#### Coarse Sample

Choose N = 32 (or 48 if you can afford it). Sample only within your cone:
- Center angle = theta0 from predicted direction \(\hat{p}\)
- Range = [-phi, +phi]

```typescript
type Sample = { theta: number; score: number; p: {x:number;y:number}; aux: Aux };

function sampleCone(A: Vec2, L: number, theta0: number, phi: number, N: number, evalAt: (p:Vec2)=>{score:number;aux:Aux}): Sample[] {
  const out: Sample[] = [];
  for (let i=0;i<N;i++){
    const u = i/(N-1);
    const theta = theta0 + (u*2-1)*phi;
    const p = { x: A.x + L*Math.cos(theta), y: A.y + L*Math.sin(theta) };
    const { score, aux } = evalAt(p);
    out.push({ theta, score, p, aux });
  }
  return out;
}
```

#### Find Local Maxima (Peaks)

A peak is where s[i] > s[i-1] and s[i] >= s[i+1] with a minimum prominence.

```typescript
function findPeaks(samples: Sample[], minProm = 0.02): number[] {
  const idx: number[] = [];
  for (let i=1;i<samples.length-1;i++){
    const a = samples[i-1].score, b = samples[i].score, c = samples[i+1].score;
    if (b > a && b >= c) {
      const prom = b - Math.max(a,c);
      if (prom >= minProm) idx.push(i);
    }
  }
  return idx;
}
```

#### Refine Peaks Cheaply (Parabolic Interpolation)

You don't need golden section. With uniformly spaced angles, a 3-point parabola gives a great refinement.

If y(-h)=a, y(0)=b, y(h)=c, peak offset:
\[\delta = \frac{h(a - c)}{2(a - 2b + c)}\]

```typescript
function refinePeak(samples: Sample[], i: number): number {
  const h = samples[i+1].theta - samples[i].theta;
  const a = samples[i-1].score, b = samples[i].score, c = samples[i+1].score;
  const denom = (a - 2*b + c);
  if (Math.abs(denom) < 1e-9) return samples[i].theta;
  const delta = (h*(a - c)) / (2*denom);
  // clamp so it doesn't jump out of bracket
  const d = Math.max(-0.9*h, Math.min(0.9*h, delta));
  return samples[i].theta + d;
}
```

Then re-evaluate only at the refined theta (one extra eval per peak).

**Result:** Per step you do ~32 evals + 2–4 extra evals. That's extremely fast.

---

### The Evaluation Function: Score + Auxiliary Measurements

Every evalAt(p) should return:
- score
- aux containing the pieces you'll want for hypothesis update:
  - tangent t
  - normal n
  - B, Q
  - signature S(p)

Keep eval deterministic and cheap: nearest-neighbor or bilinear sampling from precomputed textures/arrays.

---

### Signature Distance + "Stickiness" (The No-Edge-Jumping Law)

#### Signature Vector (Minimal But Strong)

Use a 6–8D vector:
- C: contrast across normal (luma diff magnitude)
- Pol: polarity (sign of diff)
- Q: coherence/confidence
- tx, ty: tangent direction
- optional: w: edge width proxy (difference at two radii)

```typescript
type Sig = { C:number; Pol:number; Q:number; tx:number; ty:number; W?:number };
```

#### Distance Metric

Use a weighted distance with circular handling for tangent:

For tangent similarity: use dot product cost 1 - |t·tTrack|
For polarity: penalty if sign flips (strong "edge identity" cue)
For contrast/coherence: scaled absolute differences

```typescript
function sigDist(s: Sig, track: Sig): number {
  const dt = 1 - Math.abs(s.tx*track.tx + s.ty*track.ty);
  const dPol = (Math.sign(s.Pol) === Math.sign(track.Pol)) ? 0 : 1;
  const dC = Math.abs(s.C - track.C);
  const dQ = Math.abs(s.Q - track.Q);
  const dW = (s.W!==undefined && track.W!==undefined) ? Math.abs(s.W-track.W) : 0;

  // weights: polarity and tangent dominate corridor cases
  return 2.0*dt + 1.5*dPol + 0.6*dC + 0.4*dQ + 0.3*dW;
}
```

#### Track Signature Update (EMA)

Each hypothesis maintains sigTrack as an exponentially smoothed signature:

```typescript
function emaSig(track: Sig, obs: Sig, a=0.25): Sig {
  return {
    C: track.C*(1-a) + obs.C*a,
    Pol: track.Pol*(1-a) + obs.Pol*a, // keep sign stable; can clamp later
    Q: track.Q*(1-a) + obs.Q*a,
    tx: track.tx*(1-a) + obs.tx*a,
    ty: track.ty*(1-a) + obs.ty*a,
    W: (track.W!==undefined && obs.W!==undefined) ? track.W*(1-a)+obs.W*a : track.W ?? obs.W,
  };
}
```

Polarity: you can keep Pol as a signed running value and compare by sign().

---

### Hypothesis State + Beam Update (K=3, M=3 is Enough)

#### Hypothesis Structure

```typescript
type Hyp = {
  A: Vec2;            // last committed point
  dir: Vec2;          // last step direction
  score: number;      // accumulated
  sig: Sig;           // running signature
  stick: number;      // stickiness / lock strength (0..1+)
  curv: number;       // last turning angle (for curvature continuity)
};
```

#### Candidate Generation Per Hypothesis

For each hypothesis:
1. Sample cone around predicted dir
2. Find peaks
3. Refine top peaks
4. Keep best M candidates

**Candidate object:**
```typescript
type Cand = { p:Vec2; dir:Vec2; score:number; sig:Sig; curv:number; raw:Aux };
```

#### Curvature Continuity Penalty (Don't Kink)

Let turn = angleBetween(prevDir, dir) and penalize change in turn:
\[\text{pen} = |\Delta\text{turn}|\]

```typescript
function angleBetween(a:Vec2,b:Vec2){
  const d = Math.max(-1, Math.min(1, v2.dot(v2.norm(a), v2.norm(b))));
  return Math.acos(d);
}
```

#### Switch Penalty (Identity Lock)

Define:
- sd = sigDist(sigCandidate, hyp.sig)
- switch penalty = rho * sd * hyp.stick

Where stick increases while you track confidently, decreases if confidence drops or cursor indicates lane change.

#### The Beam Step

```typescript
function beamStep(hyps: Hyp[], cursorDir: Vec2, params: Params): Hyp[] {
  const next: Hyp[] = [];

  for (const h of hyps) {
    const pred = v2.norm(v2.add(v2.mul(cursorDir, params.wv), v2.mul(h.dir, params.wd)));
    const theta0 = Math.atan2(pred.y, pred.x);

    const samples = sampleCone(h.A, params.L, theta0, params.phi, params.N, (p)=>evalAt(p, h, cursorDir, params));
    const peaks = findPeaks(samples, params.minProm);

    // rank peaks by coarse score
    peaks.sort((i,j)=>samples[j].score - samples[i].score);
    const top = peaks.slice(0, params.M);

    for (const pi of top) {
      const th = refinePeak(samples, pi);
      const p = { x: h.A.x + params.L*Math.cos(th), y: h.A.y + params.L*Math.sin(th) };

      const { score, aux } = evalAt(p, h, cursorDir, params);
      const dir = v2.norm(v2.sub(p, h.A));
      const turn = angleBetween(h.dir, dir);
      const dTurn = Math.abs(turn - h.curv);

      const sd = sigDist(aux.sig, h.sig);

      // stickiness update idea: increase if confident edge, decrease if uncertain
      const stickNext = Math.max(0, h.stick + 0.15*aux.Q - 0.10*(1-aux.Q));

      const total = h.score
        + score
        - params.mu * dTurn
        - params.rho * sd * h.stick;

      next.push({
        A: p,
        dir,
        score: total,
        sig: emaSig(h.sig, aux.sig, params.sigA),
        stick: stickNext,
        curv: turn,
      });
    }
  }

  // keep top K, but enforce diversity so you don't keep clones of same path
  next.sort((a,b)=>b.score - a.score);
  return diversify(next, params.K);
}
```

#### Diversity Filter (Prevents 3 Near-Identical Hyps)

Keep a hypothesis only if its endpoint is not too close to already kept endpoints or its signature differs enough.

```typescript
function diversify(cands: Hyp[], K: number): Hyp[] {
  const kept: Hyp[] = [];
  for (const h of cands){
    let ok = true;
    for (const k of kept){
      const dp = v2.len(v2.sub(h.A, k.A));
      if (dp < 2.0) { ok = false; break; }
    }
    if (ok) kept.push(h);
    if (kept.length >= K) break;
  }
  return kept;
}
```

---

### The "Allowed Switch" Law (No Flicker, No Surprises)

Even with beam search, you decide what becomes the committed path.

Let h0 and h1 be best two. Switch only if:
1. **Margin:** h1.score - h0.score > margin
2. **Persistence:** true for nSwitch consecutive commits
3. **User intent:** cursorDir favors h1 strongly OR user crossed normal significantly

In practice: keep activeHypId and a switchCounter. Don't change active until counter passes threshold.

---

### Commit Cadence + Preview (The FPS Secret)

#### Commit Rule

Only commit a new node when cursor distance from last committed node exceeds L.

**Between commits:**
- Compute preview using 1–2 beam steps from the current best hyp, but don't commit those points
- Draw preview as faint line

This keeps visual feedback "live" without overcommitting points.

#### Reposition/Unzip Override

As we designed:
- During unzip or backward intent: disable beam entirely
- Preview becomes straight chord
- You only resume beam when forward intent returns + a small latch

---

### Parameter Values That Behave Like a "Thinking Tool"

Good defaults:
- **L** = 8px @ 100% zoom, scale by 1/zoom
- **phi** = 60°
- **N** = 32
- **M** = 3 peaks per hyp
- **K** = 3 hyps
- **sigA** = 0.25
- **switchPersistence** = 3 commits
- **minProm** ~ 0.02–0.05 (depends on score scaling)

---

### One More Level: Making evalAt Actually "See" the Edge

If your precomputed fields are:
- B(x) edge magnitude (NMS'd)
- T(x) tangent direction
- Q(x) coherence
- plus I(x) luma/color for signature sampling

Then evalAt(p) should:
1. Sample B,Q,T
2. Compute alignment term |T·dir|
3. Compute signature by sampling ±normal
4. Return the score and aux {sig, Q, ...}

That's it.

---

## The Distilled Essence

What you're building is not "magnetic lasso." It's a local edge tracker with:

1. **Constant step size** (arc-length stability)
2. **Direction-first matching** (alignment, not rail snapping)
3. **Identity memory** (don't jump competitors)
4. **Controlled switching** (only with sustained evidence + user intent)

This specification provides the complete mathematical foundation and implementation details for a perfect magnetic lasso system that feels natural, stable, and responsive.
